meta.name: PSS-4 Perceived Stress Scale Brief
meta.description: The PSS-4 is a 4-item brief version of the Perceived Stress Scale, using items 2, 4, 5, and 10 from the PSS-10. It provides a rapid assessment of perceived stress and is suitable for situations where time is limited, such as telephone interviews or repeated measurements.
meta.author: Cohen, Kamarck & Mermelstein (1983)
meta.reference: Cohen S, Kamarck T, Mermelstein R. A global measure of perceived stress. J Health Soc Behav. 1983;24(4):385-396.
meta.instruction: The questions in this scale ask you about your feelings and thoughts during the last month. In each case, please indicate how often you felt or thought a certain way.

questions.text: In the last month, how often have you felt that you were unable to control the important things in your life?
In the last month, how often have you felt confident about your ability to handle your personal problems?
In the last month, how often have you felt that things were going your way?
In the last month, how often have you felt difficulties were piling up so high that you could not overcome them?

ranges.label: Low Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is low. You appear to be coping well with life demands.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current coping strategies
Keep up healthy habits and routines

ranges.label: Moderate Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is moderate. Consider enhancing your stress management strategies.
suggestions: Identify and address your main sources of stress

ranges.label: High Stress
ranges.description: Your perceived stress level is high. This may be affecting your well-being and warrants attention.
suggestions: Consider completing the full PSS-10 for a more detailed assessment
Consult a mental health professional for stress management support
